title: Vibe Coding Is Nonsense, But Cursor Is A Different Ballgame
author: Attila Vágó
text:
Member-only story
Vibe Coding Is Nonsense, But Cursor Is A Different Ballgame
Thoughts on writing commercial-grade code with AI and Cursor…
As a writer and patron of the arts, I have a lot to say about generative AI. None of it good. For the most part, it’s the worst thing that could happen to art and artists. The genuine usefulness of it is so incredibly limited, it makes me question the cost of running these LLMs and the destructive — note, I did not say disruptive — effects they have on our society. When it comes to software engineering, it’s a different story, though, that was very much not the case even a year ago. Coding does happen to benefit majorly from AI, and I say this as an AI skeptic.
Having tried Copilot in VsCode during its early adopter days, I look at the progress of code generation via AI, and I find myself being genuinely impressed. You see, for me, AI has to solve a problem for it to make sense. Generating Ghibli-style images was never a problem needed solving. Generating boring, factually incorrect articles was never a problem needing a solution. Nor was AI-generated music or fever-dream-like videos. Software, however, is a different story.
Solving software development challenges was always a problem searching for a solution.
